completion_promises.criterion_text: reconcile_on_boot() runs the full per-state matrix per the API-sqlite-schema.md §6 table
completion_promises.evidence: reconcile_on_boot dispatches to per-state handlers for QUEUED (no-op), PREPARING (mark INTERRUPTED + publish build-failed), RUNNING (mark INTERRUPTED), PAUSED (re-issue approval), FINALISING (mark INTERRUPTED + warning). Tests in TestPerStateRecoveryMatrix exercise every row.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PAUSED handling reads pending_approval_request_id from the build row and passes it verbatim to approval_publisher.publish(...) — the published payload's request_id matches the original
completion_promises.evidence: _handle_paused reads build.pending_approval_request_id and _build_recovery_approval_envelope sets ApprovalRequestPayload.request_id=request_id verbatim. test_paused_recovery_preserves_request_id asserts envelope.payload['request_id']=='abc-123-original-uuid'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: build paused with request_id='abc-123' → simulate crash → run reconcile_on_boot() → assert the published approval request has request_id='abc-123' (NOT a fresh UUID)
completion_promises.evidence: TestPausedRequestIdVerbatim.test_paused_recovery_preserves_request_id seeds request_id='abc-123-original-uuid', runs reconcile_on_boot, asserts envelope.payload['request_id']=='abc-123-original-uuid' and that the build_id/feature_id are also threaded through details.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PREPARING handling marks INTERRUPTED via state_machine.transition(b, INTERRUPTED, error='recoverable: ...') — never writes status directly
completion_promises.evidence: _handle_preparing calls compose_transition(Build(...), BuildState.INTERRUPTED, error='recoverable: pipeline restart during PREPARING'). TestStateMachineRouting verifies the error column is populated. TestNoRawStatusWrites static-greps recovery.py for raw 'UPDATE builds SET status' SQL — none present.

completion_promises.criterion_text: RUNNING handling marks INTERRUPTED; the next pull consumer message for that feature re-enters the lifecycle (retry-from-scratch is per-build, not per-pipeline)
completion_promises.evidence: _handle_running calls compose_transition(...) to mark INTERRUPTED. test_running_marks_interrupted asserts row status becomes INTERRUPTED and no build-failed wire event is emitted (re-pickup happens via NACK on next pull-consumer message, per API-sqlite-schema.md §6).

completion_promises.criterion_text: FINALISING handling marks INTERRUPTED with error message 'finalising-interrupted: PR may exist at <pr_url>' if pr_url was already recorded; operator reads via forge history
completion_promises.evidence: _handle_finalising sets error='finalising-interrupted: PR may exist at {pr_url}' when pr_url is set, else 'finalising-interrupted: PR creation status unknown'. TestFinalisingWarning.test_finalising_with_pr_url_records_warning and test_finalising_without_pr_url_records_unknown_warning verify both branches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Recovery is idempotent — running it twice in a row produces no additional state changes (Group D crash-recovery scenarios)
completion_promises.evidence: TestIdempotency.test_idempotent_across_full_matrix seeds one of each non-terminal state, runs reconcile_on_boot twice, snapshots and re-asserts statuses unchanged. The second-run interrupted_count==0 because PREPARING/RUNNING/FINALISING become INTERRUPTED on first run and INTERRUPTED is a no-op handler. PAUSED stays PAUSED on both runs.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Recovery never throws partial-success; if a per-state handler fails, the report records the failure but other handlers still run
completion_promises.evidence: reconcile_on_boot wraps each per-build handler in try/except; failures are appended to report.failures with (build_id, exception). TestFailureIsolation.test_one_failing_handler_does_not_block_others uses a publisher that always raises, seeds PREPARING + RUNNING, and asserts both rows still get reconciled and the failure is recorded.

completion_promises.criterion_text: RecoveryReport includes counts per state and a list of FINALISING warnings for the operator
completion_promises.evidence: RecoveryReport dataclass exposes interrupted_count, paused_reissued_count, finalising_warnings, failures, and skipped_count. TestRecoveryReport.test_report_records_all_categories seeds one of each state and asserts every category is populated correctly.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test: every Group D crash scenario (5 scenarios — preparing, running, finalising, paused, terminal-no-op) passes
completion_promises.evidence: TestPerStateRecoveryMatrix covers QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, INTERRUPTED, and terminal-no-op. TestPausedRequestIdVerbatim covers PAUSED. TestFinalisingWarning covers FINALISING. All five Group D scenarios are exercised; 15 tests in test_lifecycle_recovery.py all pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/forge/lifecycle/recovery.py tests/forge/test_lifecycle_recovery.py src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py reports 'All checks passed!'. black --target-version=py312 reports clean (files reformatted in place to match project style). mypy on recovery.py and persistence.py reports zero errors in the changed files (the one mypy error is about types-PyYAML in an unrelated config/loader.py).
